question: Define a C++ `struct Book` with members `std::string title`, `std::string author`, and `int publicationYear`. In `main`, create an array of 3 `Book` structs, populate their details, and then iterate through the array to print the details of each book.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1: Title: The Lord of the Rings, Author: J.R.R. Tolkien, Year: 1954
Book 2: Title: To Kill a Mockingbird, Author: Harper Lee, Year: 1960
Book 3: Title: The Great Gatsby, Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald, Year: 1925

errors: The struct members `t`, `a`, and `y` are not descriptive, hurting readability.
Using a C-style array `Book bks[3];` instead of `std::array<Book, 3>` is less idiomatic and potentially less safe in C++.
Using an indexed `for` loop `for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i)` instead of a range-based for loop for iterating over the array makes the code less concise and readable.
